detail: Archi has no mechanism to notify Jesse of cycle results, errors, questions, or status. Jesse cannot observe what Archi is doing or respond to it.

user_communication can only reach Jesse when he is actively watching the terminal. Archi has no way to push alerts, questions, or status updates when Jesse is away from the machine. DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and JESSE_DISCORD_ID are available in environment but unused.

Archi has no Discord integration. DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and JESSE_DISCORD_ID are present in environment. Jesse uses Discord on mobile and receives push notifications from it. This is the preferred channel for Archi to reach Jesse asynchronously.

Archi has no persistent process. It cannot receive messages from Jesse, respond to requests, or run continuously. It starts, closes gaps, and exits. Jesse cannot talk to Archi or give it direction without editing files manually.

Archi generates code that calls functions on its own modules without being able to verify those functions exist first. Needs a mechanism to introspect available module APIs before generating dependent code.

Archi can send Discord DMs but cannot receive messages from Jesse. Needs a Discord bot gateway (WebSocket) connection to listen for incoming messages and respond. The bot token DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN is already available in the environment. Use the Discord Gateway API to maintain a persistent WebSocket connection, receive MESSAGE_CREATE events for the DM channel, and route them to Archi for processing.

Archi can send Discord DMs via REST API but cannot receive messages from Jesse. Receiving Discord messages requires a persistent WebSocket connection to Discord's gateway (wss://gateway.discord.gg). The discord.py library handles this natively via commands.Bot. DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN is available. Jesse's messages should trigger Archi's generation loop or allow Jesse to give Archi direct instructions.

Archi has no mechanism to periodically reflect on whether it is actually serving Jesse well. It reacts to failures and explicit requests but never proactively asks: what do I not know about Jesse that I should? what patterns in our interactions should I be learning from? what am I doing that could be done better? what is Jesse's life like and how could I be more useful in it? Archi needs a structured self-evaluation loop that surfaces improvement opportunities from reflection, not just from failure.
